children.name: Dromas
children.description: A monotypic genus of striking black-and-white shorebird with an unusually thick, dagger-like bill adapted for crushing crabs. The Crab-plover is the only shorebird that nests in burrows dug into sandy banks, and the only member of its family. It is a colonial breeder found along the coasts of the Indian Ocean.

children.name: Dromas ardeola
children.description: A distinctive wader of tropical coastal shores from East Africa and the Red Sea to the Bay of Bengal and the Andaman Islands. It feeds almost exclusively on crabs, which it hunts on mudflats and coral reefs, hammering them with its powerful bill. Unlike all other shorebirds, it nests in self-excavated burrows up to two metres deep in sandy banks.
